raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this response to clarify my dispute regarding the XXXX suitcase purchased from XXXX XXXX in XXXX. 
I am not disputing that I made this purchase. My dispute is based on the fact that the item I received was defective, and the merchant refused to provide a refund or exchange, which is not consistent with reasonable merchant practices or consumer protection standards. 
The suitcase has a broken handle that does not retract, making it unusable for its intended purpose. I identified this defect immediately after purchase and returned to the store the following day to resolve the issue in person. 
At that time, I spoke directly with an employee and demonstrated the defect. I was toldusing a translation appthat they would not accept a return or exchange because the item was purchased as tax-free. Despite clearly showing that the item was damaged, my request was denied. I made multiple attempts to resolve the issue, including asking for an exchange, but was refused. I was effectively forced to keep a defective item. 
This refusal is unreasonable. A merchants tax-free policy does not override their responsibility to provide non-defective merchandise or to remedy defective goods. Refusing any form of resolution for a damaged product is not consistent with fair business practices. 
After returning to the United States, I made an additional attempt to resolve this directly with the merchant by contacting them. I received a response but the company stated they will not process a return so I have a broken suitcase I can not use. I have included documentation of this outreach with my dispute. 
Key Facts : Item received was defective ( non-functioning handle ) I attempted in-person return the next day and spoke with staff Merchant refused refund or exchange citing tax-free policy I made a good faith effort to resolve the issue multiple times Merchant failed to provide a satisfactory response to my outreach I was left with a damaged, unusable product and no resolution They provided false information to American Express saying I did not speak to them in person when I clearly did. 

Under American Express dispute standards, this qualifies as defective merchandise and merchant refusal to accept a valid return. The merchant failed to uphold their obligation to resolve a defective product claim. 

I respectfully request that this dispute be resolved in my favor. 
Thank you for your time and consideration.
